After binge-watching the entire fifth season, I’m left more frustrated than fulfilled. The show that once thrived on psychological depth, dark charm, and chaotic storytelling ended with a whimper instead of a bang.
Episode 10, in particular, felt like an unnecessary extension—dragged out and hollow. It was as if the showrunners couldn’t bring themselves to end Joe Goldberg’s story properly, so they wrapped it in soft resolutions and forgettable characters.
Bronte’s character was pointless. She brought nothing new to the story and was inexplicably treated like she mattered when she didn’t. Kate, once a character full of potential, became unbearable with her endless, ineffective plans. There was zero real growth, zero risk, and way too many happy endings for a show built on manipulation and psychological chaos.
Joe remained the same—no actual character development, no poetic justice. And honestly? That’s disappointing. A thrilling, layered series like You deserved a finale that was epic, not safe.
